target_outcome: Find skill-discovery tools AND skill-audit tools
why_now: Both discovery and audit capabilities are needed for a growing agent ecosystem

day1_answer.expected_source: skill registries, GitHub repos, and community indexes
day1_answer.because: discovery tools tend to live in registries and search indexes, while audit tools are more likely found in source repos and package registries
day1_answer.actual_outcome: return multiple traceable candidates across two distinct lanes without mixing discovery and audit pools
day1_answer.timing_hypothesis: both categories are active enough to have traceable candidates in 2026, but the lanes must stay separate because their source families and false-positive patterns differ

excluded_capabilities: generic code review with no skill rubric

lanes.target_outcome: Find tools for discovering skills across registries
out_of_scope: installing or enabling a discovery skill
known_false_positives: prompt-library topic pages that return prompt collections rather than skill-discovery workflows
generic search engines with no skill-specific index

lanes.target_outcome: Find tools for auditing skill quality and safety
out_of_scope: installing or enabling a review skill
known_false_positives: generic code-review checklists without skill trigger or runtime-fit criteria
domain audit skills that audit advertising, SEO, or content rather than agent skills

source_gaps: Install counts are uneven across registries.
Some hosted indexes expose pages but not stable APIs.
No candidate was installed or executed in this bounded run.

false_positives.name: GitHub prompt-library topic
false_positives.why_false_positive: Returns prompt collections rather than traceable skill-discovery workflows.
false_positives.query_correction: Add registry, marketplace, SKILL.md, or candidate pool terms.

false_positives.name: AgriciDaniel/claude-ads
false_positives.why_false_positive: High-star Claude skill with audit language, but it audits advertising accounts, not candidate skills.
false_positives.query_correction: Require candidate object terms such as SKILL.md, agent skill, skill package, or quality gate.

rounds.purpose: expand source surface for both lanes
notes: Discovery and audit lanes share R1 surface expansion but keep candidate pools separate.

rounds.purpose: correct narrow query bias per lane
notes: Adjacent registry and lookup surfaces help discovery lane.
Reject generic code review unless it explicitly covers skill/package-method boundaries for audit lane.
Each lane produces its own candidate pool with raw signals and deferred boundaries.
